blurb:
Cody came from animal control, got adopted, came back because his family couldn't keep up with him, then went through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups immersive training program. He's three years old, 33 pounds, and a Cattle Dog mix, which tells you basically everything you need to know about his energy level.

He's friendly with other dogs and proved it in a temporary foster home where he played well alongside a larger dog. He went to doggie daycare during his trial adoption and loved it. Volunteers describe him as a strong and lovable guy with a sweet nature, and he calmed right down during his bath once the pets and rubs started coming. He's mouthy in the playful way Cattle Dog mixes tend to be, not aggressive, just a dog who plays with his whole body and needs somewhere to put that.

The honest truth about Cody is that he needs more than a quiet apartment and a short walk. He needs activity, mental stimulation, and an owner who's actually going to wear him out. If that sounds like you, he will reward you with a lot of personality and a dog who is genuinely fun to be around. No toddlers given his size and energy, and cats are unknown.

Cody is available through Mostly Mutts in Marietta. Visit their website to submit an inquiry and set up a meet and greet.
shelter_hours: See mostlymutts.org for current hours and adoption appointment info
scoring_notes:
• Returned once and then put through immersive training signals a dog who has been in the system a meaningful amount of time
• High-energy Cattle Dog mix with honest caveats will resonate with active readers who feel misunderstood by generic adoption listings
• Narrower household fit — no toddlers, needs serious activity — lowers broad adoptability but makes the right match feel intentional and urgent

blurb:
Wilson came in from animal control, went through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program, landed in a foster home, and by all accounts is absolutely thriving. He's a 46-pound Bulldog/Boxer mix who is, and this is the only way to put it, a lot of fun.

He talks when he plays. Not barking, talking. Vocalizing, chatting, letting you know with full enthusiasm that he is having the time of his life. He drank from the sprayer during his bath and handed out kisses the whole time. His trainer actually uses him to help other dogs learn how to play because he's that good with other dogs. He's done well with kids of various ages in his foster home too, calm and affectionate when they come over to pet him.

A couple of things to know going in: no cats, and no toddlers who might get bowled over by an excited 46 pounds of dog. He also hasn't been around small animals without showing some prey drive on walks, so households with tiny pets probably aren't the right fit. But if you've got older kids, a dog-friendly home, and you want a dog who will genuinely make you laugh every single day, Wilson is worth a visit.

He's currently in a foster home through Mostly Mutts in Marietta. Start an inquiry on their website to set up a meet.
shelter_hours: See mostlymutts.org for current hours and adoption appointment info
scoring_notes:
• The dog who talks during playtime and drank from the bath sprayer is genuinely funny content that will stop readers mid-scroll
• Prison Pups training program adds credibility and a redemption thread that elevates a straightforward animal control origin
• Foster placement after structured training implies meaningful time in the system — enough wait-time weight to justify featuring without being the most urgent case

blurb:
Cecelia has been through a lot for a three-year-old. She was adopted from Mostly Mutts, returned after nine months, came back shut down and scared, and then slowly started figuring out that people are okay again. She's in a foster home now and doing great there. That's the version of Cecelia you'd be getting.

She's a 37-pound Terrier/Boxer mix with a whole-body wag she saves for the people she trusts. Once she trusts you, she will crawl into your lap and act like she's always lived there. She already knows sit, stay, come, and leave it through the Mostly Mutts University training program. Her foster family says she's the perfect housemate, which is a long way from where she started.

She does best as the only dog in the home. She tends to guard her person around other dogs in a house setting, though she can walk or hang out alongside dogs outside just fine. Mostly Mutts recommends calmer kids 15 and older given her history with new people. One more thing worth knowing: until she trusts you, reaching over her head or grabbing at her will spook her and she'll snap to warn you. That's not aggression, that's a dog who got scared and learned to say so. Give her time to come to you and she'll get there.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit their website to start an inquiry about Cecelia.
shelter_hours: See mostlymutts.org for current hours and adoption appointment info
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption, emotional shutdown, and slow rebuild is the most compelling individual arc in the entire candidate pool
• Specific behavioral nuances narrow the adopter pool significantly, but the right reader will feel this is written directly for them
• Adopted, returned, retrained, and still waiting — her timeline signals one of the longest shelter journeys here and adds real urgency

blurb:
Cannoli just came from animal control a few weeks ago, and he's still getting his footing. He's about 2.5 months old, roughly 2 lbs, tuxedo patterned, and currently in the shy phase that a lot of kittens go through when the whole world is brand new and a little overwhelming. The volunteers at Mostly Mutts are already putting in the time with him, and they say he's warming up fast.

He likes playing with his siblings and is starting to enjoy the petting and attention that comes his way. He's not hiding in a corner refusing to engage. He's just the kind of kitten who needs a little patience up front before he fully opens up. Give him a week or two in a quiet home and he's going to be a totally different cat.

He gets along well with other kittens, so if you're thinking about adding two, he'd probably love having a buddy. Dogs are unknown. Kids are welcome as long as they're gentle with a small kitten still figuring things out. He's litter trained, up to date on shots, microchipped, and neutered.

Cannoli is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Adoption fee is $175. Go meet him before he figures out how charming he is.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Shy-but-warming tuxedo kitten is a relatable underdog type that readers enjoy championing
• Backstory is thin — animal control origin with no distinctive arc limits narrative pull
• Explicitly noted as arriving just a few weeks ago, placing him near the bottom on wait time urgency

blurb:
Souffle came into this world in someone's garage, born to a feral mom who couldn't stick around. She made it to Mostly Mutts, settled into the Kitty Room, and then hit a rough patch with an upper respiratory infection. She's been fighting through it with a little help from the volunteers, and every day she's getting stronger. That detail alone tells you something about her.

She's about 2.5 months old, just under 2 lbs, and now that the sniffles are fading her personality is coming back online fast. She chases toys, pounces on shadows, and curls up close to purr at whoever helped her feel better that day. Sweet, gentle, and starting to rediscover what being a kitten actually feels like.

Souffle does great with other kittens, so if you already have a young cat at home who needs a buddy, she's worth meeting. Dogs and full compatibility with kids are unknown, but a calm household with patient people would suit her well while she finishes getting her strength back. She's litter trained and ready to go.

She's living in the Kitty Room at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Adoption fee is $175. Stop in and ask for Souffle.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Feral-born kitten who survived illness is a built-in emotional hook for newsletter readers
• Recovery arc from URI gives the story momentum and makes her feel like a little fighter worth rooting for
• Young kitten with recent health setback implies she arrived recently, limiting urgency on wait time

blurb:
A good Samaritan found Strawberry and her siblings out on their own without their mom when they were tiny. Too tiny. Mostly Mutts took them in, got them into foster homes, and Strawberry has been growing and thriving ever since. She's about 2.5 months old now, around 2.5 lbs, a tuxedo shorthair, and ready to find her people.

The shelter volunteers describe the whole litter as sweet and playful, and Strawberry is no exception. She's into wand toys and crinkly balls, occasionally disappears into the cat tunnel for a game of hide and seek, and then recharges on someone's lap or in a kitten pile. She knows how to have fun and she knows how to slow down. That's a good sign at 2.5 months.

She does great with other cats and would probably love having a kitten friend in her new home. Kids are welcome as long as they're gentle and know how to handle something small and quick. Dogs are unknown. She's litter trained, microchipped, and up to date on kitten shots.

Strawberry is in the Kitty Room at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Adoption fee is $175. If you've been thinking about adding a kitten to the house, she's worth the drive.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Rescued-too-young origin story paired with a thriving foster trajectory is an easy reader win
• Well-rounded personality — playful and lap-friendly — makes her one of the most broadly adoptable candidates in the set
• Foster home placement and recent age suggest moderate shelter time, not a long-wait emergency but still worth featuring
